FRACTIONAL CMO · HEALTHCARE

Fractional CMO for healthcare.

Medical practices, biotech, medtech, digital health. Healthcare marketing is shaped by regulation, payer dynamics, and trust requirements that don't apply to other sectors. The fractional CMO model works — but only with a leader who understands the compliance constraints and the way healthcare growth actually happens.

Why healthcare is different

The structural constraints

  • Compliance review on every external communication. Marketing copy goes through legal/regulatory review (especially in biotech and medtech). This adds cycle time. A fractional CMO new to healthcare will be surprised; one experienced in healthcare designs around it.
  • HIPAA + privacy constraints on data and personalization. Patient data can't be used the way consumer data can. Personalization strategies that work in DTC don't apply.
  • Payer + provider + patient triangulation. In many healthcare verticals, the patient isn't the buyer (payer is), the provider isn't the user (patient is), and the buyer doesn't experience the product (payer doesn't get treatment). Marketing has to address three audiences with different needs.
  • Long evidence cycles. Clinical evidence, peer-reviewed publications, KOL endorsement — the "content marketing" that matters in healthcare often takes 6-24 months to produce.
  • Reputational fragility. A single marketing mistake (overpromise, misleading claim, off-label suggestion) can trigger regulatory action. Stakes are higher.
Where it works

Healthcare contexts where fractional CMO fits well

  • Mid-size medical practices (specialty / multi-site). Cardiology, orthopedics, dermatology, behavioral health groups in the 5-25 provider range. Local marketing, patient experience, payer relationships, online reputation — all within scope.
  • Series A-C biotech and medtech. Building brand, investor relations support, KOL engagement, conference presence, scientific communications. Pre-commercial through early commercial.
  • Digital health startups (post-PMF). B2B (to providers/payers/employers) or DTC. Building marketing function during scale-up phase.
  • Health system service-line marketing. Often delegated to fractional or interim leadership during search for permanent service-line marketing director.
Where it doesn't

Healthcare contexts where fractional CMO is the wrong fit

  • Large health systems and academic medical centers. Marketing functions are 30-100+ people; require full-time CMO at the system or division level.
  • Pharma + biotech in late-stage commercial launch. Launch leadership requires full-time, deep MLR (medical/legal/regulatory) coordination, and full-time field/HQ commercial alignment.
  • Highly compliance-restricted segments where every campaign needs 30-day review cycles. Fractional CMO cadence doesn't fit; full-time embedded leader does.
  • Solo or 2-doctor practices. Owner-managed marketing or contractor is right; fractional is overkill.
The right operating model

What a healthcare fractional engagement looks like

  • Engagement length: Typically 12-24 months — slower compliance cycles mean longer time to demonstrate lift.
  • Reporting structure: CEO and/or chief medical officer; legal/regulatory has approval gate on external comms.
  • Tech stack discipline: HIPAA-compliant CRM, marketing automation, analytics. No exceptions. AI agent tools must support BAA (business associate agreements).
  • Content workflow: Drafting via AI-assisted infrastructure; MLR review built into the workflow, not added at the end.
  • Vendor relationships: Specialized healthcare marketing agencies for media, design, KOL engagement. Fractional CMO manages, doesn't replace.
The AI angle

Where AI helps in healthcare

  • Patient/prospect lead nurture in HIPAA-compliant agent platforms — answers FAQs, books appointments, surfaces high-intent contacts to care navigators.
  • Provider referral relationship management — proactive outreach to referring providers, content distribution, performance feedback.
  • Content production with MLR pre-screening — AI flagging likely review issues before submission speeds compliance approval.
  • Conference + KOL relationship tracking — automated CRM updates from event activity.
